name: Avery Morrow
tagline: Writer of quiet novels and occasional essays
bio:
Avery Morrow is a novelist and essayist living somewhere with too many books and not enough shelves. Their debut novel *The Quiet Sky* (Vellum Press, 2025) was longlisted for the Center for Fiction First Novel Prize and called "unmistakably alive" by the New York Times.

Before writing full-time, Avery worked as a librarian and editor. Their essays on grief, attention, and small-town America have appeared in The Paris Review, Granta, The Atlantic, and elsewhere.

This is a placeholder site. Replace this profile with your own.
